creator: gabor@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hello Torsten,

I suspect you use a chroot to run your amavisd-new installation in. Is 
that right? For me, with a default installation, the pidfile variable 
points to the right place, so the script works w/o any errors. If you 
use chroot, you have to manually modify the rc.d script. Maybe I'll 
think about providing chroot support via OPTIONS in the future, if that 
does not make things too complicated. So, please give me a feedback if 
chrooting causes this problem or not.

Regards,

-- 
Gabor Kovesdan
FreeBSD Volunteer

EMAIL: gabor@FreeBSD.org .:|:. gabor@kovesdan.org
WEB:   http://people.FreeBSD.org/~gabor .:|:. http://kovesdan.org
